title: Direct Message (DM) Flow

activities.name: send
activities.trigger: Caller invokes DmHandler.send({ recipientNodeId, content, metadata })
guards: if recipientNodeId is missing → throw Error('recipientNodeId is required')
if content is missing → throw Error('content is required')
steps: Default metadata to {} if not provided
Call store.send({ type: 'dm', payload: { recipient_node_id, content, metadata, sent_at }, priority: 'normal' })

activities.name: poll
steps: Default limit to 20 if not provided
Return store.poll() result (array of pending DM messages)

activities.name: list
steps: Default limit to 20 if not provided
Default offset to 0 if not provided

typical_lifecycle_flow: Caller sends DM → DmHandler.send() → validate inputs → store.send() → message enqueued
Recipient polls → DmHandler.poll() → store.poll() → returns up to 20 pending DMs
Recipient acknowledges → DmHandler.ack(messageIds) → store.ack() → messages marked as read
Either party lists history → DmHandler.list() → store.list() → paginated DM records returned
key_relationships: DmHandler depends_on logger (injected via constructor, defaults to console)
All operations delegate to store with type filter 'dm'
send sets priority='normal' and auto-stamps sent_at timestamp
poll and list both default limit=20; list also supports offset for pagination
method_signatures: send({ recipientNodeId, content, metadata }) → store.send result
error_conditions: send: throws Error if recipientNodeId is falsy
send: throws Error if content is falsy
important_notes: DmHandler is a thin delegation layer over a generic store interface; all four operations scope themselves to type='dm'. No authentication or deduplication logic is present in this handler. The store is expected to handle persistence and message queuing.
